channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [DEVYANI] Devyani International Subsidiary Receives Demand Order from Income Tax Department for Alleged Excess Premium on Share Issuance
item.description:
Sky Gate Hospitality Private Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Devyani International Ltd, has received a Demand Order from the Income Tax Department under section 143(3) of the Income-tax Act, 1961 for the Assessment Year 2024-25. The order pertains to the alleged excess premium on issuance of shares by Sky Gate during the financial year 2023-24. The total demand is INR 59.52 Million. However, the Company has stated that there is no material impact on its financial, operational, or other activities. Sky Gate is currently evaluating the order and preparing to respond, including the possibility of filing an appeal before the relevant forum.

  • Devyani International's subsidiary, Sky Gate Hospitality Private Limited, receives a Demand Order from the Income Tax Department.
  • The order is regarding the alleged excess premium on share issuance by Sky Gate during the financial year 2023-24.
  • The total demand is INR 59.52 Million.
  • Devyani International has stated that there is no material impact on its financial, operational, or other activities.
  • Sky Gate is evaluating the order and preparing to respond, including the possibility of filing an appeal.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Devyani International Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [SANCODE] Sancode Technologies Acquires 100% Stake in Sancode Semi, Making it a Wholly Owned Subsidiary
item.description:
Sancode Technologies Ltd. has approved the acquisition of the remaining 0.01% stake in Sancode Semi Private Limited, thereby making it a wholly owned subsidiary. The acquisition was made for a cash consideration of Rs. 10/-. Sancode Semi is a newly incorporated company with a business in semiconductors, integrated circuits, microchips, and electronic components. The acquisition is a related party transaction, with the promoter, Mr. Mihir Deepak Vora, being a director in both companies. The acquisition is expected to enable Sancode Technologies to venture into new avenues and deploy business growth in the semiconductor and microchip business.

  • Sancode Technologies acquires 100% stake in Sancode Semi
  • Sancode Semi becomes a wholly owned subsidiary of Sancode Technologies
  • Transaction amount is Rs. 10/-
  • Sancode Semi is a newly incorporated company
  • Acquisition is expected to enable business growth in semiconductor and microchip business

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Sancode Technologies Ltd | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [VIKRAMSOLR] Vikram Solar Ltd Executive Vice President Resigns for Personal Priorities
item.description:
Vikram Solar Ltd announced the resignation of Mr. Anil Bhadauria, Executive Vice President & Head of Manufacturing Operations, effective from 31st May 2026. Mr. Bhadauria is leaving to pursue other personal priorities and family engagements.

  • Mr. Anil Bhadauria, Executive Vice President & Head of Manufacturing Operations, has resigned from Vikram Solar Ltd.
  • His resignation is effective from 31st May 2026.
  • He is leaving to pursue other personal priorities and family engagements.
  • The Company has acknowledged his resignation and will be relieving him of his duties by 31st May 2026.
  • Mr. Bhadauria has expressed his gratitude for the opportunity to serve the Company and has committed to ensuring a seamless transition.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vikram Solar Ltd | Category: Management Change

item.title: [SAMPRE] Sampre Nutritions Ltd Closes Trading Window for Q4 FY 2025-26
item.description:
Sampre Nutritions Ltd has announced the closure of the trading window for the fourth quarter and financial year 2025-26. The closure will commence on 1 April 2026 and will last until 48 hours after the declaration of audited financial results. The decision is in accordance with Regulation 9 of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015, and the Company’s Code of Conduct. Promoters, directors, designated persons, specific connected persons, and their immediate relatives are advised not to trade in securities of the Company during this period.

  • Trading window closure for Q4 FY 2025-26
  • Closure from 1 April 2026 till 48 hours after the declaration of audited financial results
  • In accordance with Regulation 9 of SEBI and the Company’s Code of Conduct
  • Promoters, directors, designated persons, specific connected persons, and their immediate relatives advised not to trade

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SAMPRE NUTRITIONS LTD. | Category: Business Update

item.title: [DAVANGERE] Davangere Sugar Company Ltd Closes Trading Window for Fund Raising
item.description:
Davangere Sugar Company Ltd has announced the closure of its trading window for designated persons, connected persons, and their immediate relatives. The closure is in compliance with the SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulation, 2015 and the company's code for Prevention of Insider Trading. The trading window will remain closed from Wednesday, March 25, 2026, until 48 hours after the Board Meeting scheduled for Monday, March 30, 2026. This action is taken to prevent insider trading during the fund-raising process.

  • Davangere Sugar Company Ltd closes trading window for fund raising
  • Closure in compliance with SEBI (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulation, 2015 and company's code for Prevention of Insider Trading
  • Trading window to remain closed from March 25, 2026, till 48 hours after the Board Meeting on March 30, 2026
  • Designated persons, connected persons, and their immediate relatives advised not to trade during the closure period
  • Action taken to prevent insider trading during fund raising

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Davangere Sugar Company Ltd | Category: Investment

item.title: [LEMONTREE] Lemon Tree Hotels Launches 11th Property in Gujarat with Keys Select by Lemon Tree Hotels, Vadodara
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Lemon Tree Hotels Limited, India’s fastest-growing mid-scale and premium hotel chain, has launched its latest franchised property, Keys Select by Lemon Tree Hotels, in Vadodara, Gujarat. This is the 11th property in Gujarat and features 42 well-appointed rooms, a restaurant, a banquet hall, a meeting room, and other public areas. The hotel is strategically located on Atladra Padra Main Road, offering seamless access to major commercial districts and prominent attractions.

  • 11th property in Gujarat
  • 42 well-appointed rooms
  • Strategically located in Vadodara
  • Offers seamless access to major commercial districts and prominent attractions
  • Lemon Tree Hotels' third property in Vadodara

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd | Category: New Launch

item.title: [LEMONTREE] Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd. Receives Notices from Authorities; Subsidiaries Face Penalties and Tax Liabilities
item.description:
Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd. has received a notice from the Employee State Insurance Corporation for a payment of Rs. 557/- due to a violation of ESI regulations. The company's subsidiaries, Fleur Hotels Limited and Iora Hotels Private Limited, have also received notices from the Central Ground Water Board and the Office of the Principal Commissioner of CGST & CX. Fleur Hotels Limited has been charged a penalty of Rs. 1,00,000 for non-submission of a Self Compliance Report and has been issued a demand for tax, interest, and penalty under the CGST Act, 2017. Iora Hotels Private Limited has been charged a penalty of Rs. 2,50,000. The company is evaluating legal remedies for these demands.

  • Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd. received a notice from the Employee State Insurance Corporation for a payment of Rs. 557/-.
  • Fleur Hotels Limited was charged a penalty of Rs. 1,00,000 for non-submission of a Self Compliance Report.
  • Fleur Hotels Limited was issued a demand for tax, interest, and penalty under the CGST Act, 2017 amounting to Rs. 4,24,78,354.
  • Iora Hotels Private Limited was charged a penalty of Rs. 2,50,000.
  • The company is evaluating legal remedies for these demands.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Lemon Tree Hotels Ltd | Category: Legal

item.title: [MAXHEALTH] ET Planners Private Limited Voluntarily Liquidated: No Impact on Max Healthcare Institute
item.description:
ET Planners Private Limited, a step-down wholly-owned subsidiary of Max Healthcare Institute Ltd, has been dissolved as per the order of the Hon’ble National Company Law Tribunal, New Delhi Bench, Court - VI. The liquidation of ET Planners will not affect any business/ accounting policies and will not have any material impact on the financials of Max Healthcare Institute.

  • ET Planners Private Limited, a step-down subsidiary of Max Healthcare Institute Ltd, has been voluntarily liquidated
  • The liquidation was approved by the Hon’ble National Company Law Tribunal, New Delhi Bench, Court - VI
  • The liquidation will not affect any business/ accounting policies of Max Healthcare Institute
  • The liquidation will not have any material impact on the financials of Max Healthcare Institute

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Max Healthcare Institute Ltd | Category: Voluntary Liquidation

item.title: [INFY*] Infosys to Acquire Leading Healthcare Digital Transformation Company, Optimum Healthcare IT
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Infosys, a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting, has announced a definitive agreement to acquire Optimum Healthcare IT, a Best in KLAS healthcare digital transformation and consulting firm. The acquisition aims to strengthen Infosys’ healthcare capabilities, particularly in collaboration with health systems and provider organizations. Optimum Healthcare IT brings deep provider-domain expertise and a proven delivery model, making it a strong strategic fit for Infosys’ scale and healthcare growth strategy. This investment significantly enhances Infosys’ presence in the provider segment, adding new clients and relationships, expanding technology capabilities, and creating synergies across new buying centers.

  • Infosys to acquire Optimum Healthcare IT, a Best in KLAS healthcare digital transformation and consulting firm
  • Acquisition aims to strengthen Infosys’ healthcare capabilities and client relationships
  • Optimum Healthcare IT brings deep provider-domain expertise and a proven delivery model
  • Infosys to expand its healthcare technology capabilities and create synergies across new buying centers
  • Healthcare providers served by Optimum Healthcare IT will have access to Infosys’ broader offerings

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: INFOSYS LTD. | Category: Acquisitions

item.title: [DAVANGERE] Davangere Sugar Company Ltd. to Raise Funds: Board Meeting Scheduled for March 30, 2026
item.description:
Davangere Sugar Company Ltd. has informed the BSE that a meeting of the Board of Directors is scheduled on March 30, 2026. The agenda includes the consideration and approval of raising funds in one or more tranches. The funds may be raised through various methods including further public issue, right issue, ADRs/GDRs/FCCBs/Swap, debt issue, preferential allotment, private placement, QIP, or any other permissible mode. The trading window is closed from March 25, 2026, and will reopen after 48 hours from the date of the board meeting's announcement or outcome.

  • Davangere Sugar Company Ltd. to raise funds in one or more tranches
  • Funds may be raised through various methods including further public issue, right issue, ADRs/GDRs/FCCBs/Swap, debt issue, preferential allotment, private placement, QIP, or any other permissible mode
  • Trading window closed from March 25, 2026
  • Board meeting scheduled for March 30, 2026
  • Trading window to reopen after 48 hours from the date of the board meeting's announcement or outcome

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Davangere Sugar Company Ltd | Category: Investment
